section.title: Weekly Routine
section.p: Typically, a week covers two sections from the textbook. We discuss one section the first class meeting, and the other on the second. You should read these textbook sections and watch the embedded videos. It is best if you do this reading and watching before our class meeting. That can make the class discussion more meaningful for you. The shows you which sections are covered which weeks.

You have online homework assignments for these sections in . You access from our D2L course. The assignments are due Sunday nights at 10:00pm for full credit. They can still be worked on up until Tuesday nights at 10:00pm for reduced credit.

We spend the second half of each class meeting working on lab exercises from the PCC Calculus Lab Manual. You have options for how this goes down:
Stay seated near the front of the classroom. We will talk through exercises together. I may call upon you to share your thoughts on each exercise. Write good, complete responses to each exercise.
By the end of the week, we may not have completed all of the exercises. I will ask you to complete the remainder on your own and bring them to me no later than the following Tuesday morning. (Either in class Monday, to my office on Tuesday morning, or scanned and posted in D2L by Tuesday morning.)
You will be scored full credit for the exercises we discussed as a group. I will grade the rest separately Tuesday afternoon. I can't accept late work after I grade things Tuesday.
If you miss a class, or have to leave early some day, then I can't manage this option for you and you will need to use the last option below.
You can stay in the classroom, but remove yourself to the back. You can work alone, or perhaps with a peer as long as you are quiet enough not to distract the main group. If you are stuck on some exercise or need clarification about what a question is asking, you can signal to me and I will come help.
To earn a grade for the assignment, you must give me your complete write-up by the following Tuesday morning. (Either in class Monday, to my office on Tuesday morning, or scanned and posted in D2L by Tuesday morning.)
You can leave class at this point and write up responses to the lab questions outside of class.
To earn a grade for the assignment, you must give me your complete write-up by the following Tuesday morning. (Either in class Monday, to my office on Tuesday morning, or scanned and posted in D2L by Tuesday morning.)

In all cases, for work that you do outside of class, your peers and tutors may help you understand the exercises, but you may not have other people or computer tools effectively do the exercises for you. These lab exercises are supposed to teach you things, and you should view them as opportunities to learn, not as homework for a grade. Then you will be in great shape for our exams.

Throughout the week, consider reaching out for help in the following ways:
the Ask a Question Discussion area in D2L
contacting people from the class (if you don't have contact info, try using Connect, Classlist, in our D2L course)
free tutoring from PCC at
contacting me in office hours or over email
